items.title: Australian fears about Iran war, fuel shortages run deeper than an anti-Trump attitude

items.title: Easter trip plans cancelled over petrol price hikes driven by Middle East war
items.summary: Rising fuel prices driven by the Middle East conflict are hitting the domestic tourism sector, with cancellations surging ahead of the Easter long weekend.

items.title: How Australia became hostage to fuel imports
items.summary: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

items.title: From Australian Idol to The Piano, Guy Sebastian has tried most types of reality TV
items.summary: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

items.title: You, Me & Tuscany starring Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page marks return of the romcom
items.summary: You, Me & Tuscany is highly anticipated by fans of Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page, who can't wait for a rare romantic comedy to hit cinemas.

items.title: Australia to support NASA's Artemis II as first crewed Moon mission in 50 years launches
items.summary: Canberra-based communications systems will support NASA's first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century, helping astronauts orbit the Moon and return data.

items.title: Kangaroos' Tristan Xerri sent to AFL tribunal after alleged blood-wiping incident
items.summary: North Melbourne ruckman Tristan Xerri is sent directly to the AFL tribunal on a charge of serious misconduct after he allegedly wiped blood on Essendon captain Andy McGrath's face.

items.title: Shane Flanagan on the challenge of coaching on after son's scary neck injury
items.summary: St George Illawarra five-eighth Kyle Flanagan was transported to hospital after a head knock in the Dragons' 22-14 loss to the Gold Coast Titans.

items.title: Labor's Toby Priest defeats Liberals' Stephen Patterson to win Morphett in SA election

items.title: Australia's oldest living Olympian Gordon Ingate celebrates 100th birthday
items.summary: Gordon "Wingnut" Ingate was twice denied time off work to compete in the Olympic Games in his 20s, but continued to excel as a sailor, eventually getting the chance to compete in his 40s at Munich 1972.

items.title: Bullara Station feels 'intense' full force of category four Tropical Cyclone Narelle near Exmouth
items.summary: An outback cattle station and tourist ranch in WA's north has felt the full force of Cyclone Narelle, with vision emerging of the trail of destruction left by the powerful wind and heavy rain.

items.title: Why the 'perfect pair' of jeans remains elusive

items.title: Victorian government ordered to compensate landowner $27 million over rezoned property

items.title: Houthis open up new front in Middle East war, making things much more complex for Trump

items.title: 15-year-old Tasmanian cricketer Mia Barwick set to ride wave of women's sporting boom

items.title: Ask the Leyland Brothers' Mal Leyland planning 'one last trip' as he reveals dementia diagnosis

items.title: Hassan's family is in hospital after an Israeli airstrike, but one child will not return home
items.summary: Hassan Ali Al Jabal's family were all injured in an Israeli airstrike on their home in Lebanon. He does not know how to tell them one of his daughters is dead.

items.summary: Hundreds of complaints against Booking.com are piling up as customers and hosts allege they have lost money, their property has been trashed and no-one is taking responsibility.

items.title: In the Strait of Hormuz, ships make a bargain to negotiate their safe passage
